mission.title: Install and Configure Kured on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kured (Kubernetes Reboot Daemon) is a Kubernetes daemonset that automatically reboots nodes when necessary, ensuring safe operations by managing node cordoning and draining. Installing Kured helps maintain the health of your Kubernetes cluster by handling reboots seamlessly.

steps.title: Add the Kured Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the Kured Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add kured https://kured.dev/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Kured using Helm
steps.description:
Next, install Kured in your Kubernetes cluster using the following command. Replace `<namespace>` with your desired namespace (e.g., `kured`):
```bash
helm install kured kured/kured --namespace <namespace> --create-namespace --version 1.21.0
```

steps.title: Verify Kured installation
steps.description:
To ensure Kured is running correctly, check the status of the pods in the specified namespace:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n <namespace>
```

steps.title: Configure Kured for resource limits
steps.description:
You may want to set resource limits for Kured. Edit the Helm release with the following command:
```bash
helm upgrade kured kured/kured --namespace <namespace> --set resources.limits.cpu=100m --set resources.limits.memory=128Mi
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for Kured
steps.description:
To ensure Kured has the necessary permissions, apply the following RBAC configuration:
```yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: kured
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["nodes"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "patch"]
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
- apiGroups: ["kubelet.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["pods"]
  verbs: ["create", "delete"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: kured
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: kured
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: default
  namespace: <namespace>
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Kured will show the Kured pods running in the specified namespace, and you will have configured resource limits and RBAC permissions. Kured will now automatically manage node reboots as needed.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kured https://kured.dev/charts
helm repo update
helm upgrade kured kured/kured --namespace <namespace> --set resources.limits.cpu=100m --set resources.limits.memory=128Mi

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: kured
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["nodes"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "patch"]
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
- apiGroups: ["kubelet.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["pods"]
  verbs: ["create", "delete"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: kured
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: kured
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: default
  namespace: <namespace>

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
